items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 capital spending plans
items.summary: Mountain View is examining its capital improvement program for the next fiscal year, which outlines major infrastructure and facility projects. This study session determines where the city will invest in physical improvements like roads, parks, and public buildings.

items.headline: City Council to amend tenant protection rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing changes to its Residential Tenant Protections Program, which could affect renters' rights and landlord regulations. The ordinance amendment will be discussed at the Housing and Human Services Commission meeting on April 14.

items.headline: Youth services programs report on safety and empowerment initiatives
items.summary: City Council reviewed annual reports on youth intervention services, the Safe Summer Initiative, and the San José Youth Empowerment Alliance on April 9. These programs focus on keeping young residents safe and engaged during summer months.

items.headline: Downtown building seeks historic status upgrade and bonus floor space
items.summary: The property at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto is requesting elevation from Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource status, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for historic and seismic rehabilitation work.

items.headline: Figure AI expands San José office presence
items.summary: Figure AI is undertaking a $4.1 million second-floor office build-out at 4000 N 1st Street, signaling the AI robotics company's growing commitment to its San José operations and local workforce.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A proposal to rezone and redevelop properties at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road into an eight-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 sq ft of commercial space is under City Council review. This represents significant densification of the San Antonio Road corridor.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 has been cancelled, pushing a development project at 333 Franklin Street to a later date (April 22 or beyond). Residents interested in this project will need to watch for a new hearing notice.

items.headline: Major tech tenant improvement at Automation Park
items.summary: A $7.4 million commercial buildout is underway at 1768 Automation Parkway, the largest single project in this permit batch. This suggests significant corporate expansion or a new major tenant moving into San José's tech corridor.
